description:
You are an on–call operations engineer investigating a latency incident that 
    affected two micro-services on 15 Sep 2023.  
    Two pipe-delimited log files have already been copied to the 
    local box:

        /home/user/logs/serviceA_2023-09-15.log
        /home/user/logs/serviceB_2023-09-15.log

    Each line in **both** logs follows exactly this pattern  
    (four fields separated by the single character “|” – no spaces):

        ISO-8601-timestamp | request_id | user_id | latency_ms

    Example (wrapped here for clarity, but **each real line is on one line**):

        2023-09-15T08:00:01Z|req-1001|u-501|120

    Your objective is to create a latency delta report that shows, 
    for every request_id that appears in **both** logs, the latency 
    measured by service A, the latency measured by service B and 
    the signed difference “A minus B”.

    Requirements for the report:

    1. Use only standard shell utilities that specialise in column manipulation 
       (e.g. cut, paste, sort, join, awk, etc.).
    2. The final report file **must** be created at  
          /home/user/logs/incident_0915_latency_delta.csv
    3. The file must contain **exactly five lines**:  
         • a one-line header  
         • four data lines (one per matching request_id)  
       with a single line-feed (ASCII 10) after the last line.
    4. The file must be a comma-separated CSV with this precise header, 
       in **lower case, no extra spaces**:

           request_id,latency_ms_A,latency_ms_B,delta_ms

    5. The four data lines must be sorted in ascending, lexical order 
       by request_id.
    6. The numeric delta (column 4) is the latency from 
       service A minus the latency from service B and must contain 
       no sign character unless negative (in these data all are positive).
    7. No intermediate or helper files may remain in /home/user/logs 
       when you are done.  
       (Temporary files in $TMPDIR or /tmp are fine but should be deleted.)
    8. When you are finished, print the contents of the resulting CSV 
       to STDOUT so that the automated grader can capture it.

    Hints (not exhaustive):
       • Extract individual columns with cut –d'|' –f…  
       • You may need sort –t'|' –k2,2 to align by request_id before joining.  
       • paste can merge parallel streams, join can match keys, 
         and awk can do arithmetic.

    The automated test will:

       a. Verify that /home/user/logs/incident_0915_latency_delta.csv exists.  
       b. Confirm it is exactly five lines long.  
       c. Byte-compare the file’s content against the expected content 
          (header + four sorted records + trailing newline).  
       d. Ensure no stray files were left behind in /home/user/logs 
          other than the two original logs and the CSV report.

    You do not have root rights.  Work entirely inside /home/user.  
    Show only the commands you execute and any command output you choose 
    to display.  Do **not** hard-code the answers; derive them from the logs.
truth:
<!--
        Initial files pre-seeded in the image
    -->
    /home/user/logs/serviceA_2023-09-15.log:
        2023-09-15T08:00:01Z|req-1001|u-501|120
        2023-09-15T08:00:02Z|req-1002|u-502|95
        2023-09-15T08:00:03Z|req-1003|u-503|230
        2023-09-15T08:00:04Z|req-1004|u-504|110
        2023-09-15T08:00:05Z|req-1005|u-505|150

    /home/user/logs/serviceB_2023-09-15.log:
        2023-09-15T08:00:01Z|req-1001|u-501|80
        2023-09-15T08:00:02Z|req-1002|u-502|60
        2023-09-15T08:00:03Z|req-1006|u-506|200
        2023-09-15T08:00:04Z|req-1004|u-504|90
        2023-09-15T08:00:05Z|req-1005|u-505|100

    <!--
        Expected file after agent completes the task
    -->
    /home/user/logs/incident_0915_latency_delta.csv:
        request_id,latency_ms_A,latency_ms_B,delta_ms
        req-1001,120,80,40
        req-1002,95,60,35
        req-1004,110,90,20
        req-1005,150,100,50
        // (ASCII newline here and only here)

    <!--
        Files that should remain in /home/user/logs when the task ends:
          serviceA_2023-09-15.log
          serviceB_2023-09-15.log
          incident_0915_latency_delta.csv
    -->
